survivors_flagged.one_line: Stibnite gold-ANTIMONY (critical-mineral) build: final construction decision + EXIM $2.7B financing board vote targeted 2026, Phase-2 IDEQ permit in public comment; dated milestones with policy tailwind, but pre-production execution/financing risk caps it.
Ulixacaltamide NDA accepted, Breakthrough Therapy, hard PDUFA Jan 29 2027 for essential tremor; real dated binary but ~7mo out and largely de-risked by positive Ph3.

Live controlled-company take-private: CEO Priore (58%) bid $6.00-6.15 vs ~$15-20 SOTP, special committee active, minority shareholders (Buckley) opposing — genuine squeeze-out asymmetry; non-binding so no signed date yet.
$110B WBD acquisition (signed Feb 2026); EU Commission decision deadline July 7 2026 plus NY/CA AG litigation risk and ~7x-mkt-cap financing = complicated, dated, unresolved arb.
Two near-dated PDUFAs: Sephience/sepiapterin (PKU) July 29 2026 and vatiquinone (Friedreich's ataxia) Aug 19 2026 - stacked binary catalysts with meaningful re-rate asymmetry.
Rusfertide (polycythemia vera) PDUFA Aug/Q3 2026, Priority Review; Takeda opt-out already crystallized $200M+ cash and milestone/royalty economics that partly de-risk the binary.

Live take-private contest — Irth Capital (Brookfield-backed) $47/sh with financing secured Jun 2026 ($725M preferred + $1B+ debt) vs competing TriArtisan $65 bid after Apollo exit; dated forward bid with real asymmetry.
Live $22B Skyworks merger — exchange offers/consent solicitations on Qorvo notes launched May 20, 2026, Starboard-backed; complex un-closed deal with dated mechanics to work.
uniQure AMT-130 (Huntington's) BLA submission set for Q3 2026 — RMAT/Breakthrough, 75% cUHDRS slowing; dated regulatory binary with confirmatory-study overhang to be mispriced.

QXO building-products roll-up: Kodiak ~$2.25B definitive agreement Jan 2026 (close pending) + $3B Series C preferred committed to fund further deals; serial-acquirer optionality, near-term close ahead.
Definitive all-cash merger — Publicis $38.50/sh (~$2.5B, ~30% premium), expected close by Dec 31, 2026; antitrust/CFIUS conditions to work, live un-closed arb.
Ultragenyx has two hard dated PDUFAs — DTX401 (GSDIa) Aug 23, 2026 and UX111 (Sanfilippo A) Sep 19, 2026, both potential first-in-disease approvals; multiple imminent binaries.

Casdatifan (HIF-2a) ARC-20 RCC readouts staged 1H/mid/2H-2026 feeding PEAK-1 go/no-go, plus standing Gilead alliance/buyout optionality — one real dated binary but not yet imminent and partly de-risked after STAR-221 futility.
RP1+nivo melanoma BLA re-accepted as Class 1 with Aug 2, 2026 PDUFA plus a late-July AdCom — a hard dated binary with twice-rejected (two CRLs) asymmetry into the AdCom/decision.

ADI Global Distribution spin-off (ticker ADIG) on track for completion mid-Q3 to mid-Q4 2026; Form 10/A filed, $400M ADI notes priced Jun-16, investor days mid-July — hardening, forward, own the suppressed RemainCo/ADI.
All-stock acquisition of Valaris (15.235 RIG/VAL), expected close H2-2026; DOJ Second Request issued May-4 extends antitrust review — dated forward deal-close, RIG is acquirer.

verified.catalyst: Stibnite gold-antimony project advancing toward a final investment/construction decision in H2 2026 after EXIM financing approval, with a near-term Phase-2 IDEQ cyanidation permit pending.
verified.dated_milestone: Final investment & construction decision targeted H2 2026 (contingent on definitive EXIM loan documentation in 2H26); Phase-2 IDEQ cyanidation permit anticipated Q2 2026 (i.e. by ~June 30, 2026).
verified.bloom_gates: G2 partial (EXIM board commitment) — but already FIRED May 21, 2026; no remaining unfired G1/G2/G3 with a specific currently-unpriced figure
verified.edge: L — the headline catalyst is already public and priced/sold-off (stock $20.62 vs $37 52wk-high, ~23% below 50/200-day MAs); no informational edge remaining
verified.primary_source: Perpetua Resources / EXIM press release, "Export-Import Bank Approves $2.9 Billion Loan for Stibnite Gold Project," May 21, 2026 (prnewswire/investors.perpetuaresources.com); construction-start PR "Perpetua Resources Advances Construction of the Stibnite Gold Project" (commenced May 30, 2026); FMP quote PPTA $20.62 as of 2026-06-29
verified.analysis: The two named, dated catalysts in the triage note have already FIRED: EXIM's board unanimously approved a $2.9B (not $2.7B) senior secured loan on May 21, 2026, and critical-path construction (Burntlog Route) began May 30, 2026 after a court denied the opponents' injunction. What remains forward — a self-determined final investment/construction decision in H2 2026 (contingent on definitive EXIM docs) and a near-imminent Q2 IDEQ cyanidation permit — is real and dated but offers no named-counterparty new commitment nor a specific currently-unpriced figure, and the stock has de-rated (~$20.6 vs ~$26.7 50/200-day, $37 52wk-high) rather than re-rating, indicating financing optimism is spent and the case is now pre-production execution/dilution risk. Fails the strict forward/unpriced gate; the discrete catalyst already passed.

verified.catalyst: FDA-accepted NDA for ulixacaltamide in adult essential tremor with a hard PDUFA target action date of Jan 29, 2027 (Breakthrough Therapy, no AdCom planned).
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA target action date Jan 29, 2027 (FDA approval decision); next: any pre-decision label/CMC updates through 2H2026
verified.bloom_gates: None cleanly met. G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure) fails — the approval is largely de-risked and already massively re-rated; G1/G2 N/A (no counterparty/commitment).
verified.edge: L — positive twin Ph3 (Essential3, Oct 2025) + BTD + no AdCom make approval high-probability, and the stock has re-rated ~9x off its 52-wk low ($37→$350) into the event, so residual unpriced upside is thin; the dated binary is real but the edge is mostly spent.
verified.primary_source: Praxis IR / GlobeNewswire press release, "FDA Acceptance of NDA for Ulixacaltamide HCl," Apr 14, 2026; topline Ph3 release Oct 16, 2025
verified.analysis: PRAX's catalyst is genuine, hard-dated, and forward: FDA accepted the ulixacaltamide NDA on Apr 14, 2026 with a Jan 29, 2027 PDUFA date and no advisory committee, backed by positive twin Phase 3 Essential3 data (Oct 16, 2025) and Breakthrough Therapy (Dec 2025). However, it gates as WATCH not ACTIVE: the approval is largely de-risked and the stock trades at $350 (vs $37 52-wk low, well above its 50/200-day MAs), so the binary's upside is substantially priced in and no Bloom gate (G1/G2/G3) is cleanly cleared. The remaining edge is low — this is a dated regulatory binary to monitor into late-2026, not a currently-unpriced setup.

verified.catalyst: CEO Thomas Priore (~58% controlling holder) made a preliminary, NON-binding $6.00-6.15/share take-private bid for the minority; a special committee (Barclays / Paul, Weiss) is evaluating it amid Buckley Capital opposition citing $15-20 SOTP.
verified.dated_milestone: No dated milestone — committee has explicitly stated NO definitive timetable; no signed agreement; next hard date is undefined (would be a definitive merger agreement or committee decision, none scheduled).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named bidder = Priore) passes; G2 fails (non-binding, no signed commitment); G3 partial (SOTP $15-20 is third-party estimate, and price has already re-rated above the bid so the cheap asymmetry is largely priced out).
verified.primary_source: SEC DEF 14A / 8-K (Apr 29, 2026 proxy: "no definitive timetable," "no definitive agreement") + Buckley Capital PRNewswire statement (Nov 19, 2025); current quote $7.14 (FMP, Jun 28, 2026 close).
verified.analysis: The controlled-company squeeze-out is real and well-documented (Priore non-binding $6.00-6.15 bid, special committee with Barclays/Paul Weiss, Buckley 2.2% opposing at $15-20 SOTP), satisfying the named-counterparty gate. But it fails the strict DATED + FORWARD test: the proposal remains non-binding with no signed agreement, the committee has publicly declined to set any timetable, and there has been no take-private development since Nov 19, 2025 — a 7-month dormant stretch. Crucially the stock at $7.14 has already re-rated ~38% above the bid (from $5.19 at proposal), so the easy bid-vs-tape asymmetry is gone; what remains is an undated valuation bet on a higher revised offer or a strategic-alternatives outcome. WATCH, not ACTIVE: monitor for a revised binding bid or committee decision, which would be the actual catalyst.

verified.catalyst: EU Commission Phase 1 decision (clear-with-remedies vs Phase 2) on Paramount Skydance's $110B all-cash ($31/sh) acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery is the last major gate before a targeted Q3-2026 close.
verified.dated_milestone: EU Commission decision deadline July 7, 2026 (extendable to ~July 21 if remedies offered); note-tender financing deadline extended to July 15, 2026
verified.instrument: Cleanest expression is LONG the target WBD to capture the $31/sh cash spread; PSKY (the acquirer, flagged symbol) is a deal-completion + financing/AG-overhang bet, not a convergence-to-cash spread
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: EU Commission decision on WBD acquisition) + G2 (concrete commitment: $31.00/sh all-cash binding merger agreement, board-approved, DOJ/China/Spain already cleared)
verified.edge: M — real dated binary gate, but Reuters/Variety/Deadline report EU is leaning to clear-with-remedies (likely Universal distribution JV divestiture), so much of the spread is already priced; residual unpriced risk is CA AG Bonta investigation + private antitrust suit + ~$30B financing execution, all of which weigh on PSKY (acquirer) more than the cash-anchored WBD spread
verified.primary_source: Bloomberg "Paramount-WBD Merger Faces EU Decision by July 7" (2026-06-02) + Reuters/Variety EU clear-with-remedies reporting + DOJ closure (Washington Post 2026-06-12)
verified.kill_fact: Catalyst is real/named/dated/forward: EU Commission Phase 1 deadline is July 7, 2026 (extendable ~July 21 w/ remedies) — the last major gate after DOJ closed its probe June 12, China cleared June 17, and Spain FDI cleared June 11, 2026; deal is $31/sh all-cash (~$110B), WBD holders approved, Q3-2026 (Sept) target close — all confirmed vs Paramount press release, Bloomberg, CBS/Fox/DOJ, and the WBD/PSKY SEC filings. CORRECTION: PSKY (flagged symbol) is the ACQUIRER, not the target; the cash-convergence arb is LONG WBD to capture the $31/sh spread, so the catalyst is flagged on the wrong leg. PSKY itself is a completion/financing/litigation-overhang bet with no convergence payoff. Edge is only Medium because conditional EU clearance + Q3 close are already consensus; unpriced tail = CA AG probe, pending private HBO Max-subscriber antitrust suit, and multi-tranche debt financing.
verified.analysis: PSKY is the acquirer, not the target — the genuine arb is long WBD into the $31/sh all-cash close, so flagging PSKY puts the catalyst on the wrong leg (acquirer eats completion/financing/litigation risk without the convergence payoff). The catalyst is nonetheless real, named, and dated: the EU Commission Phase 1 deadline of July 7, 2026 (extendable to ~July 21) is the last gating approval after DOJ closed its probe June 12 and China/Spain/US cleared in June. Edge is only Medium because consensus reporting already expects conditional EU clearance and a Q3-2026 close, leaving the unpriced tail mostly in the CA AG investigation, the pending private subscriber antitrust suit (Paramount moved to dismiss June 3), and the multi-tranche debt financing whose tender deadline was pushed to July 15.

verified.catalyst: Both claimed PDUFAs already resolved in 2025 — Sephience/sepiapterin (PKU) was APPROVED July 2025 and vatiquinone (Friedreich's ataxia) got a Complete Response Letter Aug 19 2025; the only forward item is a confirmatory vatiquinone study merely initiating Q3 2026 (24-mo mFARS readout, decision years out).
verified.dated_milestone: No near-dated binary; vatiquinone open-label confirmatory study initiates Q3 2026 (24-month mFARS endpoint → readout ~2028+, resubmission later)
verified.bloom_gates: none — fails FORWARD/NOT-FIRED; both events occurred ~11 months ago
verified.edge: L — triage misdated 2025 events as 2026; market has long since priced the PKU approval (now ~$125M/qtr) and the vatiquinone rejection; no current unpriced dated figure
verified.primary_source: PR Newswire "PTC Therapeutics Receives Complete Response Letter for Vatiquinone NDA" (2025-08-19) + BioWorld "PTC's Sephience wins FDA nod for phenylketonuria" (2025-07-29) + PTC IR vatiquinone Type C meeting / Q3 2026 study-initiation update (2026)
verified.analysis: The triage thesis is factually wrong on timing: both PDUFAs were 2025, not 2026. Sephience/sepiapterin was FDA-approved for PKU around its July 29 2025 PDUFA (BioWorld, 7/29/2025) and is now generating ~$125M/quarter, while vatiquinone received a Complete Response Letter on Aug 19 2025 (PR Newswire, 8/19/2025) requiring a new adequate-and-well-controlled study. The only forward catalyst is an open-label confirmatory vatiquinone study only *initiating* in Q3 2026 with a 24-month mFARS endpoint — a study start, not a binary readout, with any resubmission/decision years away — so the claimed "stacked near-dated binary catalysts with re-rate asymmetry" no longer exist.

verified.catalyst: Rusfertide (hepcidin mimetic for polycythemia vera) Q3 2026 FDA PDUFA decision under Priority Review, with the Takeda opt-out adding $200M+$75M payable on approval plus 14-29% tiered royalties on top of the $200M already received at opt-out election.
verified.dated_milestone: FDA PDUFA approval decision, Q3 2026 (NDA accepted/Priority Review granted 2026-03-02; opt-out exercised 2026-04-28)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Takeda + concrete commitment) and G2 (concrete $200M/$200M/$75M milestone schedule + 14-29% tiered royalty); G3 only partially — the approval-conditional figure is specific and unfired but largely already priced.
verified.edge: L — the binary is real and dated but the tape has already re-rated it: ~$122 vs $111 median analyst PT, the $200M opt-out cash is fired, and a Priority-Review/Breakthrough/Fast-Track program backed by positive Phase 3 VERIFY makes Q3 approval high-probability rather than a mispriced low-base-rate bet, so the un-priced forward leg is thin.
verified.primary_source: Takeda/Protagonist press releases — opt-out terms (Yahoo Finance, 2026-04-28) and NDA acceptance/Q3 2026 PDUFA (Takeda newsroom, 2026-03-02); price/PT context (search, late June 2026)
verified.analysis: The catalyst is genuine, specific, dated and forward: a Q3 2026 PDUFA decision on rusfertide with a quantified Takeda economic package ($200M on approval + $75M milestone + 14-29% royalties + up to $975M further milestones), and the $200M opt-out payment has already crystallized cash that materially de-risks the company's balance sheet. But it scores down to WATCH/5 because most of the value is already in the price — PTGX trades ~$122 against an $111 median Wall Street price target with Strong-Buy consensus, the opt-out de-risking is itself the cause of the re-rating (the event the triage cites as bullish has largely fired), and a Priority-Review program on positive pivotal data is a high-base-rate approval rather than an unpriced asymmetry. The forward leg that remains unfired (approval-conditional payments + royalty NPV) is concrete but does not represent a clearly mispriced binary, so the asymmetry is limited.

verified.catalyst: Live two-horse take-private contest — Irth Capital (Brookfield/Morgan Stanley-financed) at $47/sh vs a competing ~$65/sh TriArtisan consortium — with the stock at $35.83, far below either bid, leaving large unpriced asymmetry pending board/merger resolution.
verified.dated_milestone: Brookfield $725M preferred + Morgan Stanley ~$1B bridge financing disclosed Jun 16, 2026; NO hard forward date set (no board-decision/vote/go-shop deadline disclosed) — next event is an undated board acceptance or definitive merger agreement.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named: Irth Capital/Brookfield/Morgan Stanley & TriArtisan); G2 (committed financing $725M preferred @12%/1.6x + ~$1B bridge, dated Jun 16 2026); G3 (price $35.83 vs $47 low bid = ~31% gap, vs $65 = ~81%, clearly unpriced)
verified.primary_source: Yahoo Finance / FMP, "Papa John's take-private bid by Irth Capital nets $725M preferred equity, $1B debt" (Jun 16, 2026); FMP quote $35.83 (Jun 29, 2026); Bloomberg "Papa John's Surges on Report Irth Capital Readies Better Bid" (Mar 11, 2026)
verified.kill_fact: The load-bearing high bid — the "~$65/sh TriArtisan consortium" that supplies the claimed "~81% under high bid" asymmetry — traces to fabricated fake-news. HunterBrook's "$PZZA Gate" report (Nov 13, 2025) explicitly alleges the $65 TriArtisan buyout offer was manufactured by a coordinated pay-to-play fake-news network (BusinessMole, ABC Money mimicking ABC News, run from Dubai), and BTIG (Peter Saleh) threw cold water on it. No primary source (SEC 8-K, company statement, named-source Bloomberg/Reuters/WSJ) confirms a real live advanced $65 TriArtisan bid as of June 2026 — only "reportedly"/unnamed-source articles descending from the discredited thread. Only the Irth $47 financing (Brookfield $725M pref, 12% all-in/1.6x, ~$1B MS bridge) is primary-confirmed (Jun 16, 2026), and even that lacks any signed merger agreement, go-shop, or board-vote milestone. The wide price-to-bid gap reflects a fictional high bid + an unconfirmed/early low bid, NOT unpriced asymmetry — the bidding-war premise is substantially fabricated.
verified.analysis: PZZA is a genuine live take-private contest: Irth Capital (Qatari-royal-backed) has committed financing disclosed Jun 16 2026 — Brookfield $725M preferred (12% all-in, 1.6x min multiple) plus ~$1B Morgan Stanley bridge — supporting a $47/sh bid, while a competing TriArtisan consortium is in advanced talks near $65/sh after Apollo pulled its ~$64 offer. With the stock at $35.83 (below yearLow-adjacent and ~31% under the LOWEST bid, ~81% under the high bid), the asymmetry is real, unpriced, forward and not yet fired — clearing Bloom G1/G2/G3. It is downgraded from the triage's 8 to 6 because no hard dated decision milestone exists (no board-vote/go-shop/deadline disclosed, only undated 'advanced discussions') and there is a material credibility overhang — a HunterBrook '$PZZA Gate' fake-buyout-news allegation and BTIG pouring 'cold water' on the reports — which the wide price-to-bid gap corroborates.

verified.catalyst: FTC antitrust clearance / merger close (early CY2027, possibly late 2026); near-term marker = exchange-offer expiry Sept 1, 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Exchange offers expire Sept 1, 2026 (S-4 effective May 29, 2026; early-participation June 11 passed); deal expected to close early CY2027, mgmt "hopeful" late 2026.
verified.instrument: QRVO common (vs short 0.960 SWKS to isolate the cash stub + spread)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named acquirer Skyworks), G2 (signed merger agreement w/ $298.7M mutual + $100M reverse termination fee), G3 (specific unpriced gross spread)
verified.edge: M — gross spread is real & idiosyncratic but thin; deal-value 0.960*$67.71+$32.50=$97.50 vs QRVO $93.54 = ~$3.96 / ~4.2% gross to an early-CY2027 close (~6-8% annualized). Spread persists because of genuine antitrust risk: horizontal #1/#2 RF/PA combination drew FTC second request + China SAMR Phase II.
verified.primary_source: Skyworks/Qorvo Form 425 + GlobeNewswire "Skyworks Commences Exchange Offers and Consent Solicitations for Qorvo's Senior Notes" (May 20, 2026); FTC second-request disclosures (Form 8-K, 2026); merger terms 0.960+$32.50 per merger agreement.
verified.kill_fact: Signed $22B SWKS/QRVO merger CONFIRMED via primary sources: 0.960 SWKS + $32.50 cash per QRVO (Qorvo PR / SWKS 8-K / Davis Polk). At live QRVO $93.54 / SWKS $67.71, implied = 0.960*67.71 + 32.50 = $97.50, gross spread $3.96 = ~4.2% — matches thesis. FTC issued a Second Request Feb 5 2026 (NOT a filed block suit — thesis correctly flags 'FTC suit' headlines as overstated); China SAMR in Phase II. Both shareholder votes passed Feb 11 2026; deal NOT closed/terminated, expected early CY2027 (mgmt hopes late 2026) — forward, not fired. CORRECTION: the Sept 1 2026 'exchange-offer expiry' is a senior-note debt-exchange procedural date (89-93% already tendered at early participation, extendable to closing), NOT the merger-close trigger — a weak near-term marker; the true fired event is regulatory clearance. Thin ~4% spread on the two largest US RF/PA suppliers combining = real horizontal antitrust + 2027-slip break risk; medium-conviction arb, score 7 fair.
verified.analysis: Genuine, dated, forward merger-arb: a signed $22B SWKS/QRVO deal with concrete consideration (0.960 SWKS + $32.50 cash = ~$97.50 implied vs QRVO $93.54), live debt exchange offers (expire Sept 1 2026), and a not-yet-fired close (expected early CY2027). The ~4% gross spread is specific and unpriced, reflecting real antitrust risk — this is the two largest US RF/PA suppliers combining, which drew an FTC second request and China SAMR Phase II review (the "FTC suit pauses deal" headlines overstate it; it is a second request, not a filed block suit). Edge is medium not high: the spread is thin, the break-risk on a horizontal RF combination is non-trivial, and the timeline could slip into 2027, so it is a moderate-conviction arb rather than a fat layup.

verified.catalyst: uniQure plans a Q3 2026 BLA submission for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease seeking accelerated approval, after the FDA (June 17 Type B meeting) agreed the 36-month Phase I/II cUHDRS/TFC data could serve as the primary basis, with a confirmatory-study design still to be aligned.
verified.dated_milestone: BLA submission targeted Q3 2026 (Jul–Sep 2026); confirmatory-study design alignment with FDA expected pre-submission.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (FDA named, Type B meeting completed 2026-06-17), G3 (statistically significant 36-mo cUHDRS/TFC slowing accepted as primary BLA basis on an accelerated-approval path; confirmatory-control overhang unresolved)
verified.edge: M — real dated regulatory binary with RMAT/Breakthrough/Fast-Track designations and FDA buy-in on data sufficiency, but the stock has already re-rated ~+75% (to $47.39 vs 50/200-day ~$27-28) on the June 17 plan, so the initial mispricing is largely fired; residual edge is whether the Q3 BLA is accepted and how the confirmatory concurrent-control study (and ~$211M raise dilution) resolve.
verified.primary_source: uniQure press release "uniQure Announces Plan for BLA Submission for AMT-130 in Huntington's Disease" / 8-K, 2026-06-17 (corroborated StockTitan & TradingView, 2026-06-17); FMP quote $47.39 as of 2026-06-29.
verified.kill_fact: PRIMARY-CONFIRMED via GlobeNewsWire PR 2026-06-17 (07:05) + SEC 8-K + FMP: after a recent Type B FDA meeting the agency agreed the 3-year Phase I/II analysis can serve as the primary basis of an accelerated-approval BLA for AMT-130 in Huntington's, which uniQure intends to submit Q3 2026; FDA wants confirmatory-study design (concurrent standard-of-care control instead of sham) aligned BEFORE submission — a reversal of FDA's March 2, 2026 stance that external-control P1/2 data were insufficient (which had cut QURE ~-33%). Forward, not-yet-fired regulatory binary, correctly dated. CORRECTIONS: (a) the PR cites 'a recent Type B meeting' / final minutes within 30 days but does not stamp the exact meeting date as June 17 (June 17 is the announcement date, not necessarily the meeting date); (b) raise magnitude understated — the upsized offering closed June 25 with ~$259M GROSS proceeds (5.69M shares at $45.50, full overallotment), so net is ~$245M, not the ~$211M cited. Spike CONFIRMED: +78.4% on June 17; live price ~$47 vs 50/200-day avg ~$27-28, so most of the binary's upside is re-rated/priced, leaving compressed but non-trivial residual edge into the Q3 filing and confirmatory-design read. Score 6 / ACTIVE holds.
verified.analysis: Confirmed real and dated: on 2026-06-17 uniQure announced a Type B FDA meeting where the agency agreed the 3-year Phase I/II analysis (statistically significant slowing on cUHDRS and TFC at 36 months) could be the primary basis for an accelerated-approval BLA, which the company intends to submit in Q3 2026 — a forward, not-yet-fired regulatory binary, not a macro bet. The overhang is genuine: FDA wants confirmatory-study design (concurrent standard-of-care control vs sham) aligned before submission, and this is a reversal from the FDA's March 2026 stance that P1/2 data were insufficient, so submission timing/acceptance carries real execution risk. The main demerit is that QURE already spiked ~+75% off the June 17 news and raised ~$211M net, so much of the binary's upside is re-rated/priced; score held at 6 (ACTIVE) reflecting a live dated catalyst with compressed but non-trivial residual edge into the Q3 filing and confirmatory-design read.

verified.analysis: The triage thesis is built on a stale milestone: the Kodiak ~$2.25B deal was definitive Feb 11, 2026 and already CLOSED April 1, 2026, so that catalyst has fired and is not forward. The genuine live, dated, unfired catalyst is QXO's acquisition of TopBuild — merger signed Apr 18, 2026, stockholders of both sides approved it today (Jun 29, ~99% QXO / ~78% BLD), with close expected on/about July 1, 2026, funded via TopBuild Series C preferred plus debt and cash. It clears Bloom G1/G2 and is forward by ~48 hours, but as an already-approved deal the close is largely priced and equity-heavy financing has de-rated QXO ($17.82 vs $20.22 200-day), so edge is low and this is a WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: LiveRamp is being acquired by Publicis Groupe for $38.50/share all-cash (~$2.5B equity value) under a signed, board-approved definitive merger agreement, with the deal still open pending shareholder vote and antitrust/CFIUS clearances.

verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Publicis Groupe), G2 (signed $38.50/sh all-cash definitive merger agreement, both boards approved), G3 (live ~$1.07/sh gross spread, ~2.9%, un-closed)
verified.edge: M — spread is real, dated, and un-closed (~2.9% gross to a ~6-mo close, ~6% annualized if Dec; less if slips to 2027); not a macro bet. But initial re-rate already occurred (price ~$37.43 vs $29 200d-avg), per-share upside is thin, and CFIUS review of a French acquirer buying a US data-identity asset plus 66 2/3% vote threshold are genuine completion risks.
verified.primary_source: LiveRamp SEC 8-K (~2026-05-16) and PREM14A (filed 2026-06-24); FMP quote 2026-06-29.
verified.kill_fact: PREM14A filed June 24, 2026 confirms a signed, unanimously board-approved definitive all-cash merger: Publicis Groupe (via Covey Merger Sub) to acquire LiveRamp at $38.50/share (~$2.546B equity, 29.8% premium to the May 15, 2026 close). Deal is live and unclosed pending a 66-2/3% shareholder vote, HSR + foreign antitrust/FDI + CFIUS clearance, expected close by Dec 31 2026, outside date May 16 2027. RAMP trades $37.43 live (FMP) vs $38.50 cash = ~$1.07 (~2.86%) gross spread. Forward, dated, tradeable merger-arb — every load-bearing fact verified against primary sources. Sub-8 because the move is largely priced and residual edge is a thin spread over a 6-month-plus close with genuine CFIUS/vote/timeline risk (modest annualized IRR).
verified.analysis: Verified definitive all-cash merger: Publicis to buy LiveRamp at $38.50/share (~$2.5B equity, ~30% premium to the May 15, 2026 close), unanimously board-approved, with PREM14A filed June 24, 2026. The deal is live and un-closed — RAMP trades $37.43, leaving a ~$1.07 (~2.9%) gross spread to be captured pending the 2026 shareholder vote (66 2/3% threshold), US/non-US antitrust, and CFIUS clearance ahead of an expected end-2026 close (outside date May 16, 2027). Qualifies as a real, dated, forward merger-arb catalyst; scored sub-8 because the bulk of the move is priced in and residual edge is a thin spread subject to genuine CFIUS/vote/timeline risk.

verified.catalyst: Two hard-dated, idiosyncratic FDA decisions on potential first-in-disease gene therapies — DTX401 (GSDIa) and UX111 (Sanfilippo A) — both still forward of today.
verified.dated_milestone: DTX401 GSDIa PDUFA Aug 23, 2026 (Priority Review, no AdComm anticipated); next/then UX111 MPS IIIA PDUFA Sep 19, 2026
verified.instrument: equity (RARE); long pre-PDUFA, calls/call-spreads straddling the Aug 23 + Sep 19 dates capture the stacked binary
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty = FDA) + G2 (concrete commitment = two accepted BLAs with assigned PDUFA action dates)
verified.edge: M — dates and first-in-disease framing are public/known, but at $30.68 (~$3.0B cap, vs 52w high $42.37 and analyst avg target $52.45) the back-to-back binaries do not appear fully priced; UX111 carries residual CRL/resubmission risk and DTX401 is a genuine coin-flip, so it is a real two-sided event, not a re-rated certainty.
verified.primary_source: Ultragenyx IR press releases — DTX401 BLA acceptance/Priority Review (PDUFA Aug 23, 2026) and UX111 BLA resubmission acceptance (PDUFA Sep 19, 2026); FMP quote $30.68 as of 2026-06-29
verified.kill_fact: Both binaries verified forward and accurately dated against primary/press sources: DTX401 (GSDIa, AAV8) PDUFA Aug 23, 2026 (Priority Review, BLA accepted Feb 23, 2026, no AdCom mentioned); UX111 (MPS IIIA, AAV9) PDUFA Sep 19, 2026 on a resubmitted accelerated-approval BLA accepted Apr 2, 2026 after a July 2025 CRL (CMC/manufacturing-inspection deficiencies). Both are potential first-in-disease gene therapies, neither fired. CORRECTIONS: cap ~$2.87B (not ~$3.0B, minor) at ~$29-30; the '$52 analyst average' could NOT be confirmed — the verified 52-week high is $42.37, so the line appears to conflate 52w high with an analyst target. Residual UX111 post-CRL resubmission/label risk and inherently two-sided approval odds remain the real disqualifiers, not date/terms error.
verified.analysis: RARE has two genuinely dated, forward, single-name binaries clustered ~4 and ~12 weeks out: DTX401 for GSDIa (PDUFA Aug 23, 2026, Priority Review, FDA does not anticipate an advisory committee) and UX111 for Sanfilippo A/MPS IIIA (PDUFA Sep 19, 2026) on a resubmitted BLA following an earlier CRL — both potential first-in-disease gene-therapy approvals. The catalyst is real and specific (counterparty FDA, concrete accepted-BLA commitments with assigned action dates) and neither has fired; at ~$3.0B cap and ~$30, materially below the 52w high and the $52 analyst average, the stacked outcome looks under-priced rather than already re-rated. This is idiosyncratic event risk, not a macro bet, so it clears the gate as ACTIVE; main caveats are the residual UX111 resubmission/label risk and the inherently two-sided nature of each approval decision.

verified.catalyst: Staged 2026 ARC-20 casdatifan (HIF-2a) RCC readouts — the mid-2026 ~45-patient casdatifan+cabozantinib IO-experienced ccRCC cohort directly reads through to the ongoing PEAK-1 Phase 3 and informs the broader go/no-go, with 1L cohorts following in 2H-2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Mid-2026 (~Q2/Q3 2026): ~45-pt casdatifan+cabozantinib ccRCC data presentation (PEAK-1 read-through); preceded by Feb-2026 monotherapy n=121 PFS update, followed by 2H-2026 1L cohorts.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete company-guided dated data presentations); G1 partial (Gilead named counterparty, 29.3% stake + option agreement) but the casdatifan opt-in already lapsed and option term ends 2026-07-14 — buyout optionality is soft/undated, not a binary.
verified.edge: M-L — catalyst is real and dated, but partly de-risked/priced: stock re-rated to $31.08 from $7.92 52-wk low (well above 200-day $21.39), the Feb monotherapy read is imminent/known, STAR-221 futility already absorbed, and the decisive PEAK-1 Phase 3 PFS readout is not a 2026 event. Gilead buyout angle is speculative, not dated.
verified.primary_source: Arcus Biosciences "Arcus Outlines 2026 Plans for Casdatifan" press release, 2026-01-08 (investors.arcusbio.com / BioSpace); Gilead option-term/29.3%-stake detail from Arcus STAR-121 Gilead-collaboration update, Q4-2025/Q1-2026.
verified.analysis: Verified via Arcus's Jan 8, 2026 pipeline-update PR: casdatifan ARC-20 readouts are explicitly staged — Feb-2026 monotherapy (n=121) PFS, mid-2026 ~45-pt cas+cabozantinib in IO-experienced ccRCC (the PEAK-1 read-through), and 2H-2026 1L cohorts incl. cas+zimberelimab — clearing G2 as a real dated forward binary. The Gilead angle is weaker than the triage implied: Gilead let its casdatifan option lapse in 2025 and its broader option term ends July 14, 2026, so the alliance/buyout thesis is soft optionality, not a dated binary. With the stock having ~4x'd off its 52-wk low and the most decisive PEAK-1 Phase 3 PFS data falling beyond 2026, the dated 2026 reads are meaningful but materially de-risked/priced — WATCH/5 holds, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: FDA accepted Replimune's third BLA resubmission of RP1 + nivolumab for advanced melanoma as a Class 1 review with an Aug 2, 2026 PDUFA goal date and a late-July 2026 advisory committee, a hard dated binary made asymmetric by two prior CRLs (Jul 2025 + Apr 2026).
verified.dated_milestone: Advisory committee meeting late July 2026; PDUFA decision Aug 2, 2026
verified.instrument: REPL equity (NASDAQ); long-dated calls / July-Aug straddle the cleaner expression for the binary given two-sided risk
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty = FDA, dated regulatory action) + G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure = the binary approve/reject + AdCom vote on a Class 1 PDUFA, twice-rejected asymmetry)
verified.edge: M — the catalyst is real, dated and unfired, but REPL has already re-rated violently (~$1.50 52wk low → $11.20, 50-DMA $6.04) on the resubmission-acceptance news, so the resubmission-accepted leg is largely priced; what stays genuinely unpriced is the AdCom vote and final approve/reject, where two prior CRLs (incl. a leadership-driven Apr-2026 reversal over contribution-of-effect) make this a true two-sided binary rather than a layup, capping rather than amplifying expected edge.
verified.primary_source: Replimune press release "FDA Acceptance of BLA Resubmission of RP1," dated June 26, 2026 (corroborated by OncLive 6/2026 "third RP1 BLA resubmission" and GlobeNewswire CRL releases 7/22/2025 and 4/10/2026)
verified.kill_fact: FDA accepted Replimune's third RP1+nivolumab melanoma BLA resubmission June 26, 2026 as a complete Class 1 response, goal date Aug 2, 2026, with a late-July AdCom — confirmed verbatim in the GlobeNewswire/company IR release. Third try after CRLs July 22, 2025 (IGNYTE not adequate/well-controlled) and April 10, 2026 (contribution-of-effect + single-arm adequacy; company cited a changed review team and no safety issue) — confirmed. Forward, hard-dated binary: the AdCom/decision is the unpriced leg; acceptance itself is now priced after the stock ran ~8x (52wk $1.50→$13.24, ~$11.63 on 06-28). CORRECTION: line's median DOR 33.7mo conflicts with the resubmission-era 24.8mo (non-load-bearing supporting stat); ORR ~33.6-34% n=140 is correct.
verified.analysis: FDA accepted the RP1 + nivolumab melanoma BLA resubmission on June 26, 2026 as a complete Class 1 response with an Aug 2, 2026 PDUFA goal date and notified Replimune to expect a late-July advisory committee — a hard dated binary supported by IGNYTE BICR ORR of 33.6% (n=140 post-PD-1) and median DOR 33.7 months. The asymmetry is real: this is the third try after CRLs in July 2025 (IGNYTE not adequate/well-controlled; population heterogeneity) and April 2026 (a new review team's leadership overruled the clinical team on contribution-of-effect despite no safety issues), so an AdCom and approval would be a meaningful de-risking against a tape that has lived through two rejections. The chief caveat is that the stock has already run ~7x off the post-CRL lows into the acceptance news, so position the catalyst around the still-unpriced AdCom/decision outcome rather than the now-priced resubmission acceptance.

verified.catalyst: Resideo's tax-free spin-off of low-margin ADI Global Distribution (ticker ADIG) into a separate public company unlocks a cleaner, higher-multiple RemainCo (Products & Solutions + ~$900M debt-funded dividend cash) that the depressed REZI tape is not pricing.
verified.dated_milestone: Investor days at NYSE Jul-13 and Jul-14, 2026; $400M ADI notes close ~Jun-30-2026; spin completion mid-Q3 to mid-Q4 2026 (escrow backstop Dec-31-2026).
verified.instrument: REZI common (pre-spin); hold through distribution to receive ADIG shares plus the re-rated RemainCo stub.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete commitment: Form 10-12B/A on file at SEC, $400M 7.125% notes priced Jun-16 closing ~Jun-30, $600M TLB + $500M RCF syndicated, board-approved tax-free separation with a hard escrow-redemption backstop) and G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: ~$900M one-time cash dividend to RemainCo Resideo plus the conglomerate-discount removal, with REZI at $30.20 vs 200d $36.11 / 52wk high $45.29 — separation value not in the tape).
verified.primary_source: PRNewswire/StockTitan "Resideo Announces Pricing of ADI's Offering of Unsecured Senior Notes" (Jun-16-2026) + SEC EDGAR ADI Global Distribution Form 10-12B/A (FY2026, CIK 0002105139); REZI quote $30.20 (FMP, Jun-29-2026).
verified.kill_fact: All four milestones verify against primary sources: $400M ADI 7.125% senior notes due Jul-15-2034 priced Jun-16-2026, closing ~Jun-30-2026 with escrow tied to the spin (PRNewswire/StockTitan); Resideo + ADI investor days Jul-13/14-2026 at NYSE; completion mid-Q3'26 to mid-Q4'26; Form 10-12B/A active on SEC EDGAR (CIK 0002105139); ~$900M cash dividend to Resideo funded by ~$1.0B new debt (notes + term facility) confirmed. Catalyst is forward (not fired) and tradeable: REZI common, NYSE, $4.6B cap, liquid. CORRECTION: thesis says REZI is "near its 52-week low at $30.20" — false. 52-week low is $21.81; at $30.20 the stock sits ~38% ABOVE its low (52-wk high $45.29). The depressed-tape framing survives only via the 200-day average ($36.11, stock ~16% below) and the 50-day ($34.06). Second gap (self-flagged): no cited sell-side sum-of-parts target; unlocked-value magnitude is inferred from conglomerate discount, not a primary analyst figure — score capped at 7, not higher.
verified.analysis: All four flagged milestones verify against primary sources: $400M ADI 7.125% senior notes due 2034 priced Jun-16-2026 closing ~Jun-30 (PRNewswire/StockTitan), investor days at NYSE Jul-13/14, completion mid-Q3 to mid-Q4 2026, and an active Form 10-12B/A on SEC EDGAR (CIK 0002105139) with a ~$900M cash dividend back to Resideo. The catalyst is real, specific, dated and forward (not yet fired and not yet re-rated — REZI is near its 52-week low at $30.20, ~16% below its 200-day average), so it clears the gate as a financing-hardened, board-committed spin with a clean dated path. Main gap: independent sell-side sum-of-parts targets were not surfaced in this focused pass, so the precise unlocked-value magnitude is inferred from the conglomerate discount rather than a cited analyst figure.

verified.catalyst: All-stock acquisition of Valaris (15.235 RIG per VAL share, ~$5.8B); HSR clock restarts 30 days after both parties substantially comply with the DOJ's May-4-2026 Second Request, with close targeted H2-2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Substantial compliance with DOJ Second Request -> 30-day HSR waiting period -> close H2-2026 (no fixed date disclosed; next public marker is the substantial-compliance certification, undated).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Valaris) + G2 (signed all-stock BCA, fixed 15.235 ratio); G3 fails on the RIG leg — the unpriced figure (~3% spread) sits in VAL, not RIG.
verified.edge: L — wrong leg + thin spread. At RIG $5.04, deal-implied VAL = $76.78 vs VAL $74.53 => only ~3.0% gross arb spread, and that spread accrues to VAL (target), not RIG (acquirer/currency). A long-RIG position captures no arb; closing instead dilutes RIG via ~15.235x share issuance. DOJ Second Request adds delay/break risk over a 6+ month horizon, making even the correct (long-VAL/short-RIG) leg low-edge.
verified.primary_source: Transocean 8-K reporting DOJ Second Request, filed via StockTitan (event dated May 4, 2026); deal terms per PREM14A (Feb 2026); spread computed from FMP RIG/VAL quotes 2026-06-29.
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real, dated and forward (signed Feb-2026 all-stock BCA at a fixed 15.235 ratio; DOJ issued a Second Request May 4, 2026, restarting the HSR clock; close targeted H2-2026), so it clears the named-counterparty/concrete-commitment gates. But it was flagged on the wrong symbol: merger-arb spread accrues to the target VAL, while RIG is the acquirer and its stock is the deal currency — a long-RIG thesis does not harvest the ~3% spread and actually faces dilution on close. The residual spread is thin (~3.0% gross over 6+ months) and burdened by Second-Request antitrust/delay risk, so as a RIG catalyst this is WATCH at best, not an actionable long.
